title: Code Evolution
subtitle: Watch code transform from chaos to clean architecture

stages.label: Telescoping Constructors
stages.description: Multiple constructors with increasing parameters. Callers pass null/false for unused options. Adding a field breaks everything.
problems: Adding new field = changing every existing call
Boolean parameters indistinguishable -- true, false, true, false

stages.label: JavaBean Setters
stages.description: No-arg constructor + setters. Readable but mutable, no completion guarantee, no validation.
problems: Object in transient invalid state during construction
No 'done' signal -- when is construction complete?
Permanently mutable -- can be modified after 'done'
Cross-field validation impossible in individual setters
stages.code:
Meal meal = new Meal();          // empty meal -- invalid state
meal.setBun("sesame");           // partial...
meal.setPattyCount(2);           // still partial...
meal.setCheese("cheddar");       // getting there...
meal.setSize("large");           // 'done'? maybe?

// But nothing stops this:
meal.setCheese("swiss");         // changed after 'done'!
meal.setBun("gluten-free");      // now gluten-free + bacon?
meal.setBacon(true);             // invalid combo -- who catches this?

stages.label: Basic Builder
stages.description: Static inner class with fluent setters. build() validates and returns a complete, valid Product. Clean, safe, extensible.
problems: More boilerplate than setters (worth it for safety)
Repeated build recipes across codebase (Director fixes this)
stages.code:
Meal meal = new Meal.Builder()
    .bun("sesame")
    .pattyCount(2)
    .cheese("cheddar")
    .size("large")
    .build();  // validates + done!

// Optional fields simply not called
Meal simple = new Meal.Builder()
    .bun("regular")
    .pattyCount(1)
    .size("small")
    .build();

// Cross-field validation in build()
// gluten-free + bacon = IllegalStateException

stages.label: Builder + Director
stages.description: Director encapsulates reusable recipes. Clients use recipe names instead of repeating build sequences.
problems: Director is optional overhead -- only add when recipes are reused
May over-abstract if recipes are trivial

stages.label: DSL (Domain Specific Language)
stages.description: Builder evolves into a mini-language. SQL builders, Gradle scripts, Kotlin DSLs are all Builders that became languages.
problems: But this is how the most elegant APIs work
